questions.q: In the reclining image of Vishnu on the cosmic ocean, which serpent serves as his resting couch?
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe Vishnu reclining upon the coils of the thousand-headed serpent Shesha (Ananta).

questions.q: When Vishnu reclines upon Shesha in cosmic slumber, by what name is he most often addressed in this form?
questions.explanation: Reclining on the waters, Vishnu is invoked as Narayana, 'he who moves upon the waters.'

questions.q: From which part of the reclining Vishnu's body does a lotus rise, bearing Brahma to begin a new creation?
questions.explanation: In the Puranas a lotus rises from Vishnu's navel, and upon it Brahma is born to begin creation.

questions.q: Which goddess is depicted attending at the feet of the reclining Vishnu?
questions.explanation: Lakshmi, Vishnu's consort, is shown attending at his feet in the reclining Narayana image.

questions.q: Upon what cosmic body of water does Vishnu recline on Shesha between cycles of creation?
questions.explanation: Vishnu reclines upon the cosmic ocean of milk, the Kshira Sagara, in the Puranic image.

questions.q: The deep cosmic slumber of Vishnu reclining on Shesha is traditionally called by which term?
questions.explanation: His cosmic slumber upon Shesha is termed yoga nidra, the yogic sleep of Narayana.

questions.q: Which deity, born from the navel-lotus of the reclining Vishnu, takes up the work of creation?
questions.explanation: Brahma, born on the lotus from Vishnu's navel, begins the new creation in the Puranic account.

questions.q: The thousand-headed serpent Shesha, on whom Vishnu reclines, is also widely known by which other name?
questions.explanation: Shesha is also called Ananta, 'the endless one,' reflecting his role beyond the cycles of time.

questions.q: The reclining form of Vishnu on Shesha is most commonly known by which devotional title?
questions.explanation: This reclining form is called Anantashayana, 'reclining upon Ananta (Shesha).'

questions.q: Vishnu, who reclines upon Shesha, is one of the three great deities of which triad?
questions.explanation: Vishnu, the preserver, is part of the Trimurti alongside Brahma the creator and Shiva the destroyer.

questions.q: In the cosmic order of the Trimurti, what role does Vishnu, reclining on Shesha, primarily fulfil?
questions.explanation: Vishnu is the preserver of the cosmos, sustaining order through the cycles of creation and dissolution.

questions.q: What is the name of the divine bird that serves as Vishnu's vahana (mount)?
questions.explanation: Garuda, the eagle, is the vahana of Vishnu and the traditional foe of serpents.

questions.q: Which conch is held by Vishnu among the emblems he bears in his four hands?
questions.explanation: Vishnu's conch is named Panchajanya, one of his four principal emblems.

questions.q: What is the name of the spinning discus weapon carried by Vishnu?
questions.explanation: Vishnu wields the Sudarshana Chakra, his radiant discus weapon.

questions.q: The reclining of Vishnu on Shesha occurs during the interval between which two cosmic events?
questions.explanation: He reclines in yoga nidra during the pause between the dissolution (pralaya) and the next creation.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, the serpent Shesha continues to support which cosmic structure even between the worlds?
questions.explanation: Shesha is said to bear the worlds and the earth upon his hoods, enduring through the ages.

questions.q: Besides Lakshmi, which goddess is sometimes named as a consort of Vishnu, associated with the earth?
questions.explanation: Vishnu is often shown with Shridevi (Lakshmi) and Bhudevi, the goddess of the earth.

questions.q: In which class of texts is the image of Vishnu reclining on Shesha most fully elaborated?
questions.explanation: The Puranas, such as the Vishnu and Bhagavata Puranas, elaborate the reclining Narayana imagery.

questions.q: The mace borne by Vishnu among his four emblems carries which name?
questions.explanation: Kaumodaki is the name of the mace (gada) carried by Vishnu.

questions.q: The rhythm depicted by Vishnu's reclining slumber and the rising lotus chiefly symbolises which idea?
questions.explanation: The image depicts the cosmic rhythm of dissolution followed by renewal of creation.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, the cosmic Vishnu reclining on the waters from whom creation unfolds is described as which supreme being?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana describes the reclining Narayana as the primeval Purusha from whom the cosmos emerges.

questions.q: In iconography, how many heads or hoods is the serpent Shesha most traditionally said to bear?
questions.explanation: Shesha is the thousand-headed serpent, his many hoods forming a canopy over the reclining Vishnu.

questions.q: According to Puranic accounts, two demons that arose during Vishnu's cosmic sleep threatened Brahma on the navel-lotus. By what names are they known?
questions.explanation: The demons Madhu and Kaitabha arose during Vishnu's cosmic sleep and were slain by him to protect creation.

questions.q: Because he slew the demons Madhu and Kaitabha during his cosmic repose, Vishnu earns which epithet?
questions.explanation: Vishnu is called Madhusudana, 'slayer of Madhu,' for destroying the demon during his cosmic sleep.

questions.q: The serpent Shesha on whom Vishnu reclines is, in the Puranas, counted as a son of which sage and his serpent-mother?
questions.explanation: Shesha is named among the serpent sons of the sage Kashyapa and Kadru in Puranic genealogy.

questions.q: The serpent Shesha is traditionally believed to have incarnated on earth as which companion of an avatar of Vishnu?
questions.explanation: In the Ramayana tradition, Shesha is said to have incarnated as Lakshmana, the devoted brother of Rama.

questions.q: The famous reclining Vishnu sculpture in the Dashavatara temple, depicting Anantashayana, is found at which ancient Gupta-era site?
questions.explanation: The celebrated Anantashayana relief is carved at the Gupta-period Dashavatara temple at Deogarh.

questions.q: The great reclining Vishnu (Padmanabhaswamy) enshrined at a famous temple in Thiruvananthapuram derives the name 'Padmanabha' from which feature?
options: The lotus emerging from his navel
questions.explanation: 'Padmanabha' means 'lotus-naveled,' referring to the lotus rising from the reclining Vishnu's navel.

questions.q: In Vaishnava cosmology, the form of Vishnu reclining on Shesha upon the cosmic ocean is identified with which of the Purusha manifestations?
questions.explanation: Vaishnava texts identify the reclining Narayana with the Purusha forms such as Kshirodakashayi Vishnu lying on the ocean.

questions.q: In Puranic reckoning, the duration of one of Vishnu's cosmic nights of slumber upon Shesha corresponds to which span?
options: A kalpa, one day of Brahma
questions.explanation: Vishnu's cosmic repose spans a kalpa, equal to one day (and night) of Brahma in Puranic time-reckoning.
